line 159-160: unsafeParent root slash cleanup — mutation to not strip trailing slash breaks prefix check for paths like /root vs /root-dir
line 165: unsafeParent equality check — mutation == to != breaks parent==root case
line 167-169: unsafeParent prefix+slash validation — mutations to these bounds break safe-root enforcement; removing the [root_clean.len] == '/' check allows escapes
line 173: unsafeParent outside-root return true — mutation to false bypasses root confinement
line 177-179: unsafeParent root symlink check — mutation to skip this allows symlinked roots
line 184-185: cur_buf overflow guard — mutation to >= allows buffer overrun
line 190: component overflow check — mutation to >= allows overflow in path reconstruction
line 217: isSymlink check before write — mutation to remove enables symlink-target writes
line 223-224: re-check after mkdir — mutation to remove allows TOCTOU symlink race
line 238: O_EXCL exclusive mode — mutation to remove enables clobber vulnerability (including symlink clobber)
line 239: mode 0o644 file perms — mutation changes perm bits, kills the mode assertion
line 307: lstatExists — mutation to follow symlinks breaks symlink detection
line 308: isSymlinkPath — mutation to remove enables symlink writes
line 309: target_exists check — mutation to ! inverts non-file detection
line 310: unsafeParent check — mutation to remove bypasses parent safety

line 410: agent.is_default filter — mutation to remove includes explicit-only agents in defaults
line 423-425: agentMatches call in loop — mutation to remove breaks alias resolution
line 434: dedup seen check — mutation to remove enables duplicate agent targets
line 489: --only lookahead startsWith('--') — mutation to lookahead index or bounds allows consuming non-value arg

hotspots: line 52: startsWith("---") — kill by changing to startsWith("--") or removing check entirely; NOT killed by existing tests if the check is inverted (e.g., !startsWith would return .frontmatter="", .body=text either way, so assert must check WHICH path is taken)

line 54: open_end < text.len condition before indexing text[open_end] — off-by-one mutations (open_end + 1, open_end - 1) survive if text has exactly 3-char prefix "---"; existing tests do NOT check boundary at EOF
line 55: text[open_end] != '\n' — replacing '!=' with '==' silently swallows the fence and returns empty; existing test at line 376 catches trailing space but NOT other non-LF characters after CR

line 63: indexOfPos(u8, text, search, "\n---") — mutation to indexOfPos(u8, text, search, "---") (missing \n) or changing search start point; existing tests do NOT verify that search correctly advances past false positives
line 70: nl + 4 (past "\n---") — off-by-one mutations (nl + 3, nl + 5) are NOT caught; existing tests do NOT verify byte-exact positions when the closing fence is matched
line 72-73: after < text.len checks and text[after] == '\r' — removing the after < text.len boundary check causes out-of-bounds read; existing tests do NOT exercise this boundary
line 73: text[after] == '\n' — replacing '==' with '!=' causes false closers to be accepted; NOT killed if the preceding CR check is also mutated
line 77: search = nl + 1 — changing to search = nl + 2 or search = nl causes infinite loop or missed closers; NOT killed because tests do NOT verify the full loop count
line 110: posixBasename result — off-by-one in the segment iteration loop; tests pass valid basenames but do NOT verify pathological separator edge cases

line 119: std.ascii.eqlIgnoreCase(seg, comp) — changing to std.mem.eql (case-sensitive) silently fails for .SSH vs .ssh; test line 413 catches \.GnuPG but mutation could swap eql/eqlIgnoreCase GLOBALLY
line 172: exact eqlIgnoreCase in dotPrefixArm — mutation to std.mem.eql (case-sensitive) survives if tests only pass lower-case basenames
line 173-174: base.len + 1 boundary check — off-by-one mutations (+ 0, + 2) or removing the check; NOT killed if name.len is tested near boundaries
line 174: eqlIgnoreCase(name[0..base.len], base) — replacing with eql (case-sensitive); test at line 402 (UPPER.KEY) catches extension arm but NOT basename arms for case sensitivity
line 188: algo.len >= rest[0..algo.len] bounds — off-by-one in algo.len calculation; NOT killed because tests pass fixed known keys (id_rsa, id_ed25519) without boundary variants
line 191: std.ascii.eqlIgnoreCase(tail, ".pub") — mutation to eql (case-sensitive) survives if test only passes ".pub" not ".PUB"

line 37: if (n <= 0) return — SILENT FAIL: mutation (n <= 0) → (n < 0) lets broken writes leak; NO test catches partial write or write failure
line 56: const nl = std.mem.indexOfScalarPos(u8, data, pos, '\n') orelse break — mutation orelse break → orelse error.BadMessage would detect truncated header; current tests assume well-formed input
line 59: orelse return error.BadHeader — mutation (sp != null) → (sp == null) by removing orelse; tests do NOT verify rejection of space-less headers
line 61: std.fmt.parseInt(usize, header[sp + 1 ..], 10) — no error handling visible; if parse fails, error bubbles to main caller; NO test provides invalid count
line 62: if (pos + count > data.len) return error.ShortPayload — boundary check; mutation (>) → (>=) would reject zero-length payload; NO test exercises empty count=0

line 66-70, 71-74, 75-78: three parallel dispatch arms with no mutual exclusion — mutation std.mem.eql → std.mem.startsWith would allow prefix matches (fm matches fm+extra); NO test uses ambiguous names
line 73: try out.print(gpa, "{d}\n", .{res.len}) where res is string literal "1" or "0" — mutation could swap these; tests on isSensitivePath side, not CLI output format
line 84: writeAllStdout(out.items) with no allocation check — if ArrayList.append failed silently (gpa exhausted), out could be incomplete; NO test exercises allocator failure
line 44: defer gpa.free(data) — if readAllStdin error, data is uninitialized; mutation of readAllStdin to fail early would leak or crash; NO test exercises read failure path

tests.name: e2e_full_protocol_round_trip_multiple_messages
tests.asserts:
main processes multiple request messages in one stdin stream: 'fm 10
hello---
' + 'sens 4
.env' + 'strip 15
```
body
```'; all three produce correct outputs to stdout without state leakage between messages
tests.killsMutation: line 53: pos += count — if position tracking broken, loop reads garbage or skips messages; test verifies all three outputs present

hotspots: line 26: if (n < 0) return error.ReadFailed — mutation (n < 0) → (n <= 0) would catch premature EOF as error; current tests do NOT exercise n==0 path
line 37: if (n <= 0) return — SILENT FAIL: mutation (n <= 0) → (n < 0) lets broken writes leak; NO test catches partial write or write failure
line 56: const nl = std.mem.indexOfScalarPos(u8, data, pos, '\n') orelse break — mutation orelse break → orelse error.BadMessage would detect truncated header; current tests assume well-formed input
line 59: orelse return error.BadHeader — mutation (sp != null) → (sp == null) by removing orelse; tests do NOT verify rejection of space-less headers
line 61: std.fmt.parseInt(usize, header[sp + 1 ..], 10) — no error handling visible; if parse fails, error bubbles to main caller; NO test provides invalid count
line 62: if (pos + count > data.len) return error.ShortPayload — boundary check; mutation (>) → (>=) would reject zero-length payload; NO test exercises empty count=0

line 66-70, 71-74, 75-78: three parallel dispatch arms with no mutual exclusion — mutation std.mem.eql → std.mem.startsWith would allow prefix matches (fm matches fm+extra); NO test uses ambiguous names
line 73: try out.print(gpa, "{d}\n", .{res.len}) where res is string literal "1" or "0" — mutation could swap these; tests on isSensitivePath side, not CLI output format
line 84: writeAllStdout(out.items) with no allocation check — if ArrayList.append failed silently (gpa exhausted), out could be incomplete; NO test exercises allocator failure
line 44: defer gpa.free(data) — if readAllStdin error, data is uninitialized; mutation of readAllStdin to fail early would leak or crash; NO test exercises read failure path
